nameMeanings.name: Pope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pope (derived via Old English from Late Latin papa ‘bishop, pope’, from Greek pappas ‘father’, in origin a nursery word.) In the early Christian Church, the Latin term was at first used as a title of respect for male clergy of every rank, but in the Western Church it gradually came to be restricted to bishops, and then only to the bishop of Rome; in the Eastern Church it continued to be used of all priests (see Popov , Papas ). The nickname would have been used for a vain or pompous man, or for someone who had played the part of the pope in a pageant or play. The surname is also present in Ireland and Scotland.

variants.name: Popov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Nikolay, Oleg, Grigoriy, Anatoly, Andrei, Arkadi, Dmitriy, Mikhail, Sergey.  Bulgarian or Macedonian Atanas, Dimitar, Yordan.  Serbian Bosko, Bozidar, Branko.

Russian, Bulgarian, Macedonian, Serbian, and Rusyn: patronymic from pop ‘priest’, from Greek pappas ‘father’ (compare English Pope ). The name may sometimes derive from a nickname, but celibacy was not enjoined on priests of the Orthodox Church and so the name can indeed mean ‘son of the priest’. In many cases, however, the Russian name means no more than ‘of the priest’ and designates a servant of a priest. Compare Popoff , see also Pop 2, Popovic , and Popovski 1.
Jewish (American): most likely a shortened form of   Popovski   2. Compare   Popoff  .

variants.name: Papke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Otto, Willi.
North German (also Päpke): nickname from a diminutive of Low German pape, Dutch paepe ‘pope’. Compare Pope .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When George Henry Pope was born in 1400, in Suffolk, England, his father, John Pope II, was 25 and his mother, Mary Spencer, was 22. He married Joan Everard in 1424, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1490, in Hampshire, England, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Henry Pope est né en 1400, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Pope II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mary Spencer, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Joan Everard en 1424, à Mildenhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1490, à Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Henry Pope 1400 in Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Pope II 25 und seine Mutter Mary Spencer 22. Er hat 1424 in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Joan Everard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Hampshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Henry Pope è nato il nel 1400 nel Suffolk, England, il suo padre, John Pope II, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mary Spencer, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Joan Everard nel 1424, nel Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1490, nel Hampshire, England, all'età di 90 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: George Henry Popeが1400年にイギリススフォルクで生まれた時，父John Pope IIは25歳，母Mary Spencerは22歳であった。1424年にイギリスイギリススフォルクミルデンハルでJoan Everardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1490年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで90歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: George Henry Pope(이)가 1400년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Pope II(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Spencer(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1424년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 미데홀에서 Joan Everard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 영국 함프실르에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Joan Everard em 1424, em Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1490, em Hampshire, Inglaterra, com 90 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Henry Pope родился в 1400 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John Pope II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Spencer, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Everard в 1424 г. в местности маилденолл, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности хампшир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Henry Pope nació en 1400, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Pope II, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mary Spencer, tenía 22 años.
